abort_connection.reason: Strict Phase-4 policy has an explicit post-commit connection-abort path; transport-level canonical evidence is pending.

client_abort.reason: No canonical Apache client-abort lifecycle case is implemented.

config_inline_rules.reason: The modsecurity_rules directive loads inline rules through libmodsecurity; canonical config-load evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: The modsecurity_rules_remote directive is wired to libmodsecurity; no remote-network capability is asserted by the no-CRS baseline.

config_rules_file.reason: The modsecurity_rules_file directive loads local rules files; canonical positive and negative config evidence is pending.

connection_metadata.reason: The native request hooks pass Apache client and server addresses and ports to libmodsecurity; no current canonical capability result is attached.

content_type_scope.reason: SecResponseBodyMimeType selects libModSecurity inspection, but the connector gates every response because the C API cannot expose the effective MIME decision; the deprecated legacy MIME file cannot narrow the gate. Evidence is pending.

deny.reason: Disruptive ModSecurity statuses are returned through the Apache hook/filter path; canonical deny evidence is pending.

drop.reason: The Apache intervention mapper does not preserve a distinct ModSecurity drop action.

event_jsonl.reason: A bounded common JSONL event serializer is used for Phase-4 interventions, but request-phase event coverage must still be exercised.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: Apache intentionally does not release an original first byte before first EOS because the all-response Phase-4 decision must complete before output release.

header_limits.reason: No canonical Apache host-header-limit enforcement case is implemented in the full-lifecycle catalog.

http1_chunked.reason: Apache filter wiring can receive chunked output, but no canonical transport result is attached.

http1_content_length.reason: Apache can serve HTTP/1.1 responses through the all-response Phase-4 gate; the focused H1 run has not yet produced canonical run-scoped evidence.

http2.reason: No Apache HTTP/2 full-lifecycle result is attached; ci/runtime/lifecycle/run-apache-phase4-response-regression.sh is the task-local H1/H2 evidence placeholder, not a pass claim.

http2_cleartext_h2c.reason: The pinned Apache harness has no h2c prior-knowledge listener.

http2_downstream.reason: The pinned Apache native-host profile does not yet build and exercise mod_http2 on the connector path.

http2_multiplexing.reason: No native Apache HTTP/2 multiplexing isolation evidence path is implemented.

http2_stream_reset.reason: The Apache strict path only models a connection abort; no request-local HTTP/2 stream-reset API is implemented.

http2_tls_alpn.reason: The pinned Apache harness has no TLS/ALPN h2 listener.

http2_upstream.reason: No Apache native connector upstream HTTP/2 profile is implemented.

http3_alt_svc.reason: No native Apache HTTP/3 Alt-Svc profile is configured.

http3_downstream.reason: The pinned Apache native host has no audited HTTP/3 module, QUIC listener, or H3 stream path.

http3_multiplexing.reason: No native Apache HTTP/3 stream-isolation evidence path is implemented.

http3_quic.reason: The pinned Apache native host has no QUIC runtime path.

http3_stream_reset.reason: No Apache H3 stream-reset API has been audited or wired.

http3_upstream.reason: No Apache native connector upstream HTTP/3 profile is implemented.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached.

late_intervention.reason: Late-intervention policy remains a defensive branch for independently proven already-committed output; normal Phase-4 enforcement occurs before original response release. Behavioral evidence is pending.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The configured strict late-intervention branch has a controlled connection-abort path for independently committed output, but no canonical real-host event proves actual abort_connection and connection_aborted=true.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The configured safe late-intervention branch preserves independently committed output and records a log-only result, but it is not the normal all-response-gate deny path; canonical event evidence is pending.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: Phase-4 metadata wiring exists, but no canonical event yet proves separate requested WAF status, original host status, visible client status, requested action, and actual action.

log_only.reason: Non-disruptive rules remain allowed and Phase-4 policy can record log-only outcomes; canonical evidence is pending.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: Apache intentionally retains the normalized response brigade across calls through first EOS to enforce the all-response Phase-4 decision before original output release.

parallel_requests.reason: The full-lifecycle parallel-request evidence path is not implemented.

phase1.reason: The early request hook processes URI and request headers before request handling; the canonical Phase-1 baseline has not been run here.

phase2.reason: The late request hook and input filter complete request-body processing; canonical Phase-2 evidence is pending.

phase3.reason: The output filter invokes response-header processing before body processing; canonical Phase-3 evidence is pending.

phase4.reason: The output filter incrementally ingests body buckets, retains the complete normalized response through first EOS, and invokes response-body processing once before releasing or discarding original output; canonical evidence is pending.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: The output filter invokes response-body processing once after the first retained EOS and resolves intervention before its saved original brigade is released; canonical Phase-4 evidence is pending.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: The output filter retains original response bytes and EOS until msc_process_response_body and intervention resolution complete; a normal deny discards the saved brigade and enters one terminal error path before release. Current real-host evidence is pending.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The native output-filter path is wired for incremental ingestion, retained-brigade EOS processing, and a pre-release decision, but no current canonical real-host run proves that Phase-4 rule 1100301 was evaluated.

protocol_first_byte_before_response_end.reason: The all-response Phase-4 gate intentionally prevents original first-byte release before EOS on every protocol profile; no progressive-output protocol capability is claimed.

protocol_no_full_response_buffering.reason: The all-response Phase-4 gate intentionally retains the normalized response brigade through EOS; no no-full-buffer protocol capability is claimed.

protocol_transaction_isolation.reason: Protocol-specific multiplexed transaction-isolation evidence is not implemented.

redirect.reason: The intervention mapper sets Apache Location output and returns supported redirect statuses; canonical redirect evidence is pending.

request_body_buffered.reason: The Apache input filter borrows each current request bucket and completes request-body processing at EOS; canonical body evidence is pending.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The input filter borrows and appends each Apache bucket and finalizes Phase 2 only at EOS; a real-host capability run is pending.

request_body_limits.reason: No connector-local configurable request-body limit action is implemented for the Apache streaming filter.

request_body_streaming.reason: The connector appends body chunks but completes the ModSecurity body phase as a buffered phase rather than exposing streaming phase semantics.

request_headers.reason: The native module maps Apache request headers and calls the ModSecurity request-header phase; canonical baseline evidence is pending.

response_body_buffered.reason: The output filter appends response bytes incrementally and retains the normalized Apache response brigade through first EOS before it resolves Phase 4; canonical Phase-4 evidence is pending.

response_body_decompression.reason: The connector has no verified response-body decompression contract.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The output filter appends each data bucket incrementally while retaining the normalized brigade across filter calls through first EOS; runtime evidence is pending.

response_body_limits.reason: The configurable default 1048576-byte Phase-4 gate limit and fixed non-configurable 4096-normalized-bucket cap across filter calls reject their respective excess before any original byte is released rather than forwarding an uninspected tail; canonical limit evidence is pending.

response_body_streaming.reason: Apache deliberately withholds original response output until first EOS and the Phase-4 decision, so it does not provide client-visible progressive response streaming.

response_headers.reason: The Apache output filter maps response headers and invokes the response-header phase; canonical Phase-3 evidence is pending.

transaction_id.reason: Static and Apache-expression transaction IDs are wired into transaction creation; canonical event correlation evidence is pending.

transport_metadata.reason: The native filters retain status and commit metadata, but canonical transport evidence is pending.

upstream_abort.reason: No canonical Apache upstream-abort lifecycle case is implemented.

compile.reason: An APXS module build path exists; no current canonical compile result is attached.

config_load.reason: The harness has a real httpd configuration-test path; no current canonical result is attached.

crs_smoke.reason: Legacy limited CRS smoke wiring exists and is not baseline evidence.

extended_matrix.reason: Extended-matrix verification is outside this canonical no-CRS declaration.

link.reason: The APXS path links the module with libmodsecurity and common sources; no current canonical link result is attached.

minimal_runtime_smoke.reason: A real-host request harness exists; no current canonical minimal-runtime result is attached.

no_crs_baseline.reason: The native host path can consume the shared no-CRS rules and cases, but no canonical baseline result is attached.

source_contract.reason: Native source, metadata, configuration, and harness wiring are present; the canonical source-contract check has not yet produced run evidence.

start_smoke.reason: The harness can start httpd without a request; no current canonical result is attached.

host_model_constraints: Apache incrementally appends response data buckets but saves every normalized response brigade in the request pool through first EOS; no original response byte is released before msc_process_response_body and intervention resolution complete.

libModSecurity's C API does not expose its effective SecResponseBodyMimeType selection, so Apache gates every response MIME type. The legacy modsecurity_phase4_content_types_file parser is deprecated and cannot narrow that gate; the configurable default 1048576-byte gate limit and a fixed non-configurable 4096-normalized-bucket cap across filter calls fail closed rather than releasing an uninspected tail.

Normal r->prev and pre-output ErrorDocument redirects fail closed because the connector cannot safely rebind a source transaction to a target URI/ruleset. During terminal output EMITTING, exactly one Apache-core-marked local ErrorDocument hop is allowed with no_local_copy plus matching immediate predecessor status and REDIRECT_STATUS.
The connector JSONL writer is currently specific to Phase-4 interventions and does not by itself prove canonical event coverage for request-phase decisions.

A normal Phase-4 deny discards the saved original brigade and emits a terminal error before release. log_only and abort_connection remain defensive fallbacks only when independent commit proof already exists.

abort_connection.reason: The external authorization service cannot directly abort Envoy's downstream connection as a ModSecurity action.

client_abort.reason: The external authorization service cannot observe or directly control an Envoy downstream client-abort lifecycle.

config_inline_rules.reason: The repository HTTP authorization service configuration parser and Common Runtime support inline rules; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: Common Runtime exposes remote rule key and URL configuration, but the no-CRS baseline does not exercise external networking.

config_rules_file.reason: The checked-in service path loads a local rules file through Common Runtime; canonical config evidence is pending.

connection_metadata.reason: The HTTP authorization service maps its local Envoy peer socket rather than the original downstream connection metadata.

content_type_scope.reason: The selected ext_authz path does not receive an upstream response Content-Type or response body to scope.

deny.reason: A disruptive Common Runtime decision is returned as a non-2xx ext_authz response; a fresh canonical deny result is pending.

drop.reason: The HTTP authorization contract returns an authorization response and cannot directly silent-drop the downstream connection.

event_jsonl.reason: Common Runtime writes bounded metadata-only disruptive-decision JSONL; canonical field and payload-absence evidence is pending.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: The request-only ext_authz service cannot observe an Envoy client first byte or upstream response end.

header_limits.reason: The ext_authz header allowlist is not a connector-local canonical header-limit enforcement contract.

http1_chunked.reason: Envoy can proxy chunked HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical ext_authz transport case covers them.

http1_content_length.reason: Envoy can proxy HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical ext_authz transport case covers Content-Length handling.

http2.reason: Envoy HTTP/2 transport behavior is not exercised by a canonical ext_authz full-lifecycle case.

http2_cleartext_h2c.reason: No ext_proc h2c listener is configured.

http2_downstream.reason: The selected native ext_proc harness has no TLS/ALPN HTTP/2 listener profile.

http2_multiplexing.reason: No ext_proc HTTP/2 stream-isolation runner is implemented.

http2_stream_reset.reason: Closing an ext_proc gRPC stream is not a client-visible HTTP/2 reset and no native reset hook is wired.

http2_tls_alpn.reason: No pinned ext_proc TLS/ALPN listener is configured.

http2_upstream.reason: No ext_proc upstream HTTP/2 evidence profile is implemented.

http3_alt_svc.reason: No Envoy H3 Alt-Svc profile is configured.

http3_downstream.reason: The selected native ext_proc harness has no QUIC/HTTP/3 downstream listener.

http3_multiplexing.reason: No Envoy H3 stream-isolation runner is implemented.

http3_quic.reason: No Envoy QUIC listener is configured in the pinned native harness.

http3_stream_reset.reason: No client-visible QUIC stream-reset hook is implemented for ext_proc.

http3_upstream.reason: No ext_proc upstream HTTP/3 evidence profile is implemented.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached to the Envoy ext_authz path.

late_intervention.reason: The authorization decision completes before the upstream response starts, so there is no response-phase late intervention point.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The selected pre-upstream ext_authz host model has no upstream response-phase point from which the authorization service can perform a late connection abort.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The selected pre-upstream ext_authz host model has no committed upstream response on which to apply a late log-only intervention.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: The selected ext_authz integration does not observe an upstream response, so it cannot produce Phase-4 metadata that distinguishes original and visible upstream-response status or late actions.

log_only.reason: A non-disruptive rule leaves the authorization response successful; explicit canonical log-only evidence is pending.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: The request-only ext_authz host model has no upstream response stream for this property to describe.

parallel_requests.reason: The Envoy ext_authz full-lifecycle catalog has no parallel-request evidence path.

phase1.reason: The authorization service maps request headers into the Common Runtime Phase-1 path; a canonical baseline result is pending.

phase2.reason: Buffered request-body forwarding and Common Runtime Phase 2 are configured, but no existing host smoke proves that Envoy delivered the body.

phase3.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz call cannot inspect upstream response headers.

phase4.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz call cannot inspect upstream response bodies.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: HTTP ext_authz never receives the later upstream response stream or its end-of-stream event.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: Because ext_authz never sees the later upstream response, it has no Phase-4 response point at which to deny before the response commits.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The selected Envoy ext_authz integration executes before the upstream response and does not expose upstream response-body data for Phase-4 rule evaluation.

protocol_first_byte_before_response_end.reason: No negotiated-protocol first-byte barrier is implemented.

protocol_no_full_response_buffering.reason: No negotiated-protocol no-full-buffer proof is implemented.

protocol_transaction_isolation.reason: No multiplexed downstream protocol transaction-isolation evidence is implemented.

redirect.reason: The Common Runtime preserves redirect status and URL, but the repository HTTP authorization response does not emit the Location header.

request_body_buffered.reason: Envoy with_request_body and the connector buffered body mode are both configured at 4096 bytes, but the existing host smoke sends no body.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The selected ext_authz integration uses a bounded buffered authorization body, not incremental request-body ingestion.

request_body_limits.reason: Envoy with_request_body is configured with a 4096-byte bound, but no canonical host limit case has exercised it.

request_body_streaming.reason: The selected ext_authz integration deliberately uses the with_request_body buffered authorization request, not a streaming body protocol.

request_headers.reason: The ext_authz HTTP request forwards an allowlisted header set into the Common Runtime mapper; canonical header coverage is pending.

response_body_buffered.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive the later upstream response body.

response_body_decompression.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body to decompress.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz service never receives an upstream response-body stream.

response_body_limits.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body on which to enforce a response-body limit.

response_body_streaming.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body stream.

response_headers.reason: HTTP ext_authz runs before the upstream request and does not receive the later upstream response headers.

transaction_id.reason: The configured x-request-id header is resolved by Common Runtime and included in decision events; canonical correlation evidence is pending.

transport_metadata.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz service does not observe client-visible upstream-response commitment or transport outcomes.

upstream_abort.reason: The pre-upstream authorization service cannot observe or control an upstream response-abort lifecycle.

host_model_constraints: The selected Envoy HTTP ext_authz service observes a pre-upstream authorization request and cannot inspect the later upstream response headers or body.
The checked-in ext_authz configuration buffers at most 4096 request-body bytes with partial messages disabled; the existing minimal smoke sends no request body.
Only the headers allowed by the ext_authz authorization_request configuration are forwarded to the authorization service.
The authorization service currently sees the Envoy-to-service socket endpoints and does not map original downstream connection endpoints into Common connection metadata.

response_body_limits.reason: No selected HAProxy response-body path exists on which to enforce a response-body limit.

host_model_constraints: HAProxy and the repository-owned SPOP agent are separate processes; host and agent liveness and logs are distinct evidence.
Response phases run only when the HAProxy configuration sends the corresponding response notification to the agent.
The selected SPOE/SPOP configuration sends response headers only; the prior wait-for-body response sample is deliberately disabled because it is not a low-latency response stream.
Starter and binding self-tests are not HAProxy host-runtime evidence.

The full-lifecycle profile separately selects native-htx-filter through full-lifecycle-haproxy-htx: a patched HAProxy 3.2.21 non-promoted path with real P1/P2/P3 response outcomes and P4 safe log_only evidence. Its one-block P2 probe returns 403 and records zero or one observed upstream requests without proving their ordering; that does not prove incremental request forwarding or a general host-buffering property. P4 Strict has no client-visible abort evidence and remains NOT EXECUTED.

external_references.title: Traefik Proxy v3.7 ForwardAuth configuration

host_model_constraints: The selected forwardAuth service observes the authorization request before the upstream request and cannot inspect the later upstream response headers or body.

Traefik v3.7 supports buffered forwardAuth request bodies with forwardBody and maxBodySize, but the checked-in dynamic configuration does not enable them and the service config sets request_body_mode=none.
Traefik documents that forwardBody buffering breaks streaming, so streaming request-body inspection is outside this host path.
The authorization service currently sees the Traefik-to-service socket endpoints and does not map original client connection endpoints into Common connection metadata.

The full-lifecycle profile separately selects native-middleware through full-lifecycle-traefik-native in a pinned Traefik local-plugin host probe. Its selected UDS engine service performs Common/libmodsecurity evaluation, while this forwardAuth compatibility declaration remains separate and unpromoted.
